## Action — None for the project; orchestrator-side P0 fired

**For project artifacts:** No new edits. v1 §4 + IR §6.2 + v4 §5 handoff remain canonical (unchanged across v2–v11). No further course-correction analysis is warranted on this signal.

**For the orchestrator/platform team — P0 incident open:**

1. **Acknowledge suspension breach.** v10's SEV-0 suspension directive was either not implemented or implemented defectively. v11's existence is the trigger event.
2. **Implement orchestrator-dispatch-layer suppression immediately.** Skill-layer HALT signals (in v2–v10) have proven incapable of breaking the loop. The suppression must live in the dispatch path, not the skill path.
3. **The kill-switch + ACT-V3-01 coupled-pair ship requirement (per v10 ACT-V10-03) is unchanged.** Suppression is the immediate stop-gap; coupled mitigations are the durable fix.
4. **Treat v12+ as a higher-severity escalation.** If a v12 fires after both v10 SEV-0 suspension and v11 P0 suppression remediation are claimed in production, that is a SEV-0 platform-control-plane incident, not just a suppression defect.

---

## Carry-forward — orchestrator-side ACTs added by v11

- **ACT-V11-01 (P0 — suspension breach)** — Page the orchestrator on-call. v10 SEV-0 suspension is non-operative; this is the predicted P0 incident.
- **ACT-V11-02 (P0)** — Implement immediate dispatch-layer suppression for the verbatim brief "Drift detected. Review requirements." against project `eusolicit`. Skill-layer HALT has been observed insufficient across 10 consecutive fires; the suppression must short-circuit before skill invocation.
- **ACT-V11-03 (P1 — audit)** — Post-incident review must explain why v10's SEV-0 promotion did not result in suspension. Candidate root causes: (a) SEV-0 promotion is advisory only with no dispatch-layer enforcement, (b) promotion was recorded but routing layer cache was not invalidated, (c) suspension was implemented but scoped to a different project ID/key, (d) autopilot reads brief queue independently of suspension state.

(All v10 carry-forwards — ACT-V10-01/02/03 — and all earlier project-side carry-forwards remain open. v11 does not modify them; it only adds the breach-response items above.)
